strategy.char_limit_note: 200 characters is an absolute hard limit on LinkedIn free accounts. Count every character including spaces, punctuation, and name at the start. Test in a notepad before sending.
strategy.tone: Slightly awkward genuine observation — the kind a real person makes when they noticed something but are not sure how to say it perfectly. Stands out from polished AI slop by being specific and slightly imperfect.
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by'
Do not use 'I noticed your impressive work'
Do not mention AI or automation
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity and connection only
Do not use 'reach out', 'touch base', or 'synergies'
Do not mention 'digital marketing', 'web design', 'SEO', or 'website' explicitly
Do not reference the email outreach — treat as a cold connection from someone who noticed their business
Do not use generic 'I love what you do' phrases
Do not start with 'I' — start with the person's name or business
what_works: Start with their name — feels personal, cuts through
Name the specific business — shows you looked, not just searched
One specific observation — not vague, not flattering, just real
End with low-pressure framing ('in case it's useful', 'connecting in case relevant')
Slightly awkward construction is fine — it reads human
strategy.priority_upgrade_rule: HANDS: always check LinkedIn profile for posts from the last 60 days BEFORE sending any note. If post found: write bespoke post_reference note (3-4x higher acceptance rate). If no recent posts: use pre-generated generic note.
strategy.batch_5_note_rule: Batch 5 notes must NOT reference the email sequence, campaign, or any specific website audit finding. Treat as cold LinkedIn connection from someone who noticed their business through general research.
strategy.post_reference_priority_for_batch_5: Solicitors > Accountants > Hotels/B&Bs > Restaurants > Care providers > Trades

ssl_pas_leads.sector_note: Generate note referencing the specific business, not the SSL issue. Do not mention 'Not Secure', SSL, HTTPS, or website in the note.
ssl_pas_leads.generic_template: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [sector] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.
ssl_pas_leads.post_reference_template: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] caught my attention looking at [sector] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's relevant.
ssl_pas_leads.char_count_generic: ~150-170 chars (varies by name/business/town length). Always count before sending.
ssl_pas_leads.char_count_post_ref: ~170-195 chars depending on post topic length. Tight — count carefully.

mobile_pas_leads.generic_template: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [sector] in [town]. Noticed something about how the site works on phones — worth a quick mention. Connecting in case useful.
mobile_pas_leads.post_reference_template: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] caught my attention looking at [sector] businesses in [town]. Something about the online presence caught my eye. Connecting in case relevant.
mobile_pas_leads.char_count_note: Adjust for name/business name length — 200 char hard limit. Always count.

no_website_bab_leads.note: These leads have no website — note focuses on local presence and being easy to find online.
no_website_bab_leads.generic_template: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [sector] businesses around [town]. Something about local online visibility caught my attention. Connecting in case it's worth a conversation.
no_website_bab_leads.post_reference_template: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] came up looking at [sector] businesses around [town]. Something about local visibility caught my attention. Connecting in case useful.
no_website_bab_leads.char_count_note: Slightly shorter generic — 'local online visibility' phrasing uses characters efficiently. Count carefully.

high_value_aida_leads.generic_template: [First name] — [Business name] has a strong reputation around [town]. Noticed something about how easy you are to find online — worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.
high_value_aida_leads.post_reference_template: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] has a strong local reputation. Something about the online visibility caught my attention. Connecting in case relevant.
high_value_aida_leads.char_count_note: Keep well under 200 chars — these leads have high-quality profiles with more scrutiny.
